moves.label: Subtract 5 from both sides
moves.note: Peel from the outside in — the +5 comes off first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 2
moves.note: Totally legal! But now everything's a fraction. Peeling the +5 off first keeps it clean.

moves.label: Subtract 11 from both sides
moves.note: Legal, but it just made a big zero — x isn't any freer.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 2
moves.note: x was MULTIPLIED by 2, so ÷2 is the undo.

moves.label: Subtract 2 from both sides
moves.note: x is MULTIPLIED by 2 — the undo is ÷2, not −2. Undo pairs matter!

moves.label: Distribute the 4/3 into the bracket
moves.note: Open the package: 4/3 multiplies BOTH things inside. (Psst: 7 = 7/1.)

moves.label: Multiply both sides by 3
moves.note: Ooh, the fraction-killer move! ×3 wipes out the /3 everywhere.

moves.label: Add x to both sides
moves.note: Legal! But x is still locked inside the bracket. Open the package first.

moves.label: Add x to both sides
moves.note: Gathering all the x's on one side — that's the game.

moves.label: Add 28/3 to both sides
moves.note: Legal — but x still lives on BOTH sides. Herd the x's together first.

moves.label: Combine the x terms  (x is 3x/3 in disguise)
moves.note: Costume change! x = 3x/3, so 8x/3 + 3x/3 = 11x/3.

moves.label: Subtract 9 from both sides
moves.note: Legal, but a zero on one side doesn't free x. Tidy the x-pile first.

moves.label: Add 28/3 to both sides
moves.note: Outside-in: clear the −28/3 before touching the ×11/3.

moves.label: Multiply both sides by 3/11 now
moves.note: Legal! But it smeared 3/11 over EVERYTHING. Peel the −28/3 off first — outside-in.

moves.label: Make 9 into thirds (9 = 27/3) and add
moves.note: Another costume: 9 = 27/3. Now the right side is one tidy fraction.

moves.label: Multiply both sides by 3/11 right away
moves.note: That's true! But typing THAT into a calculator is how fingers slip. Combine the pile first — it comes out beautifully.

moves.label: Multiply both sides by 3/11 (the flip!)
moves.note: To undo ×11/3, multiply by its reciprocal 3/11. The 55/3 melts into 5.

moves.label: Multiply both sides by 11/3
moves.note: That wrapped x TIGHTER! To undo ×11/3, use its flip: 3/11.

moves.label: Distribute the 4
moves.note: No fractions in sight. This path is smooth.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 4
moves.note: Legal, but it re-invited fractions to the party. Distribute instead.

moves.label: Add 3x to both sides
moves.note: All the x's, one side. 8x + 3x = 11x.

moves.label: Subtract 8x from both sides
moves.note: Legal! It works — but it leaves x with a minus sign to wrestle. Adding 3x is friendlier.

moves.label: Add 28 to both sides
moves.note: Outside-in: the −28 peels off first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 11 now
moves.note: Legal, but it smeared /11 over everything. Clear the −28 first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 11
moves.note: 11 × 5 = 55. Clean landing.

moves.label: Subtract 11 from both sides
moves.note: x is MULTIPLIED by 11 — the undo is ÷11, not −11.

moves.label: Distribute the 3 into (−x + 2)
moves.note: Deepest bracket first — untie the innermost knot.

moves.label: Just drop all the brackets
moves.note: Careful! A minus in front of a bracket flips signs when it opens. Brackets aren't erasable — they're openable.

moves.label: Add the two 3s at the ends
moves.note: Legal! But the tangle is INSIDE. Onions peel from the middle out here.

moves.label: Combine x + (−3x)
moves.note: x − 3x = −2x. One forward, three back: you land on minus two.

moves.label: Combine 2x with the x deeper inside
moves.note: Those two x's live in different rooms (bracket levels) — you can't combine through a wall.

moves.label: Open the minus-bracket: flip BOTH signs
moves.note: −(−2x + 6) = +2x − 6. The minus flips every sign inside — no survivors.

moves.label: Open it without flipping
moves.note: The minus outside flips EVERYTHING inside: −(−2x + 6) becomes +2x − 6, not −2x + 6.

moves.label: Combine everything
moves.note: 2x + 2x = 4x, and 3 − 6 + 3 = 0. The numbers vanish entirely!

moves.label: Combine just the 2x + 2x
moves.note: Good start — now finish the job: 3 − 6 + 3 = 0.

moves.label: Combine −3x + x
moves.note: −3x + x = −2x. (Remember: x is 1x in disguise.)

moves.label: Subtract 5 from both sides
moves.note: Also works! Clearing the plain numbers first.

moves.label: Add 3x to both sides
moves.note: Legal! But now x is on both sides and we're playing ping-pong. Combine the like terms that are already together.

moves.label: Subtract 5 from both sides
moves.note: The 5 peels off, leaving the −2x alone.

moves.label: Divide both sides by −2 now
moves.note: Legal, but it smeared −2 over everything including the 5. Peel the +5 first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by −2
moves.note: Divide by the WHOLE coefficient — minus sign included. 4 ÷ (−2) = −2.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 2
moves.note: Sooo close — but that leaves −x, and you'd still need one more flip. Divide by −2 and finish in one move.

moves.label: Combine −3x + x
moves.note: −3x + x = −2x. Both roads meet here.

moves.label: Add 3x to both sides
moves.note: Legal, but the x's just switched rooms again. Combine them — they're already next to each other.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 3
moves.note: The WHOLE bracket is ×3 — one move peels it all off. Elegant.

moves.label: Distribute the 3
moves.note: Also perfectly good! Open the package and work from there.

moves.label: Subtract 2 from both sides
moves.note: That +2 is INSIDE the wrapper — you can't reach it until the ×3 is off (or the bracket is open).

moves.label: Subtract 2 from both sides
moves.note: And x stands alone.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 2
moves.note: Legal, but x isn't multiplied by 2 here — the 2 is ADDED. Undo adding with subtracting.

moves.label: Subtract 6 from both sides
moves.note: Outside-in: the +6 goes first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 3 now
moves.note: Actually — that works great too! (It's the same as the shortcut path.) But since it lands in the same place, let's call subtracting 6 the move here.

moves.label: Subtract 3 from both sides
moves.note: x is MULTIPLIED by 3 — undo with ÷3, not −3.

moves.label: Open the bracket: the minus flips BOTH signs
moves.note: −(x − 4) = −x + 4. Every sign inside gets flipped — no exceptions.

moves.label: Multiply both sides by −1
moves.note: Bold! One ×(−1) and the minus is gone from the whole left side.

moves.label: Open it, flipping only the x
moves.note: The minus hits EVERYTHING inside: the x AND the −4. It's −x + 4.

moves.label: Subtract 4 from both sides
moves.note: Peel the +4, and only −x remains.

moves.label: Add x to both sides
moves.note: Legal — and honestly it works! But today's drill is staring down the −x. Face it head-on.

moves.label: Multiply both sides by −1
moves.note: −x means −1·x, so one more ×(−1) sets x free. Both sides flip: x = −6.

moves.label: Subtract x from both sides
moves.note: Legal but it DOUBLED the tangle. −x just needs one sign flip: ×(−1).

moves.label: Multiply both sides by −1 again
moves.note: That just flipped us back to the start! One flip was enough.

lines: Left side: 9.  9 = 9 ✓ (Ugly answers check out just like pretty ones.)

moves.label: Distribute the −5 (flip signs as it goes!)
moves.note: −5 hits BOTH things inside: −5·3x = −15x, and −5·(−1) = +5. The minus flips the sign.

moves.label: Distribute the 5 without its minus
moves.note: The minus is part of the −5 — it rides along and flips the −1 into +5.

moves.label: Add 1 to both sides
moves.note: That −1 is INSIDE the wrapper — you can't reach it from out here.

moves.label: Subtract 5 from both sides
moves.note: Peel the +5 off; the x-term stands alone.

moves.label: Divide both sides by −15 now
moves.note: Legal! But it smeared −15 over everything. Peel the +5 first — outside-in.

moves.label: Divide both sides by −15
moves.note: Divide by the WHOLE coefficient, minus included. 4/(−15) = −4/15. Yes, it's ugly. It's still right.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 15
moves.note: Almost — but that leaves −x and one more flip. Take the −15 in one bite.

title: Y, in terms of x
goal: Get y by itself (in terms of x)

lines: Our answer: y = 4 − (4/5)x. At x = 5:  y = 4 − 4 = 0
The answer KEEPS the x — that's the whole point. y depends on x.

moves.label: Subtract 4x from both sides
moves.note: y is the VIP here — everything that isn't y gets escorted to the other side. The 4x comes along as a package; it doesn't become a number.

moves.label: Subtract 20 from both sides
moves.note: Legal, but a big zero doesn't free y. Move the things AWAY from y instead.

moves.label: Divide everything by 5 first
moves.note: Legal, and honestly almost there! But dividing while 4x still sits next to y sprays fractions early. Move the 4x first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 5 (ALL of both sides)
moves.note: The ÷5 hits every term: 20÷5 = 4 and 4x÷5 = (4/5)x. The x stays in the answer — it's supposed to!

moves.label: Subtract 5 from both sides
moves.note: y is MULTIPLIED by 5 — the undo is ÷5, not −5.

moves.label: Open −(x − 3): flip BOTH signs
moves.note: −(x − 3) = −x + 3. The minus flips everything inside.

moves.label: Open it as −x − 3
moves.note: The minus hits the −3 too: −(x − 3) = −x PLUS 3.

moves.label: Distribute the 9
moves.note: 9·(−x) = −9x and 9·1 = 9. Every resident of the bracket gets multiplied.

moves.label: Combine 8 − x + 3 with the 4x across the equals
moves.note: Whoa — terms can't jump across the = sign by themselves! Combine only what lives on the same side.

moves.label: Tidy both sides (combine like terms)
moves.note: Left: 8+3 = 11. Right: 4x − 9x = −5x. (One forward, nine back = five back.)

moves.label: Subtract 8 from both sides
moves.note: Legal! But both sides are still messy. Tidy up BEFORE moving things — less to carry.

moves.label: Add 5x to both sides
moves.note: −x + 5x = 4x. Gathering the x's on the friendlier (positive) side.

moves.label: Add x to both sides
moves.note: Legal, and it works! But it parks the x's with a minus sign. Adding 5x keeps them positive — friendlier.

moves.label: Subtract 11 from both sides
moves.note: 9 − 11 = −2. Negative numbers are allowed to show up — let them.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 4 now
moves.note: Legal but it fractioned the 11 too. Peel the +11 off first.

moves.label: Divide both sides by 4
moves.note: −2/4 = −1/2. Simplify the fraction and take a bow.

moves.label: Add 2 to both sides
moves.note: x is MULTIPLIED by 4 — undo with ÷4.
